item.title: Grog Guitars Epoxy River Atlas 5 w/Deluxe Gig Bag — $5,199.99
item.description:
$5,199.99 · Brand New · Indy String Theory LLC

This bass features a live edge Maple and epoxy river top and back, and an Ayous wood body. It has a maple and Mahogany neck, with a Santos Rosewood scarf joint and fretboard. The body is finished with high gloss polyester, and the backside of the neck has a satin finish.

The Atlas is outfitted with black hardware, with Hipshot Triple Lock Single Saddles and Hipshot USA Ultra Light Tuners. It also features Grover style locking strap buttons, and a black tusk neck.

There are EMG MM5CS pickups in both the neck and bridge positions, and the controls include a master volume, a pickup blend, and two stacked EQ knobs.

This bass comes with a Gator deluxe gig bag, and weighs 8.8 lbs. It also requires a 9 volt battery.

Here are some specs from the Grog website:

"Model Atlas

Number of Strings 5

Weight 8lbs 13oz

Scale Length 33in-35in fan, 5th fret perpendicular

String Spacing 17mm

No. of Frets 24

Fret Material Stainless

Fret Size .047x.104

Finish Polyester High Gloss"

item.title: Squier Classic Vibe Jaguar Bass, Laurel Fingerboard, 3-Color Sunburst (L2) (05582) — $369
item.description:
$369 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Weight - 8lb 14oz

Up for sale is a Fender Squier Classic Vibe Jaguar bass in 3 color sunburst finish. This one came to us in great shape and plays fantastic. The electronics have been play tested and are working as they should. This one may have some very light surface scratching, but it is in overall excellent condition. Case not included.

The Classic Vibe Jaguar Bass turns up the volume on retro style, combining the iconic body shape of the Jaguar with the massive tone of its Fender-Designed alnico pickups. Player-friendly features include a medium 32 scale length, a slim, comfortable C-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets as well as a vintage-style bridge with slotted barrel saddles.

This throwback Squier model also features 1970s-inspired headstock markings, rich-looking nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tint gloss neck finish for an old-school vibe.


SKU:0374560500


Franklin Guitar Works - Who We Are
FGW is a division of MIRC, LLC (Musical Instrument Reclamation Company), located in La Vergne, TN.  MIRC is the world’s largest refurbisher of stringed instruments.  We partner with the leading manufacturers in the industry, helping them recondition their distressed instruments. All of our instruments have been inspected, set up, and refurbished by our certified technicians to ensure you are receiving a quality instrument.

Hours of Operation
Our online Business hours are Monday - Friday 7:30 am to 4:00 pm CST. Closed on weekends and holidays.

Shipping Policy
Free ground shipping within the continental USA. Items under $20 will have a $9.95 shipping charge. We do not offer international shipping.
We cannot ship to a PO Box. Orders require a physical mailing address to be processed.
All orders over $500 will be shipped with a direct Signature Requirement to ensure it is received. If the signature requirement is refused, the customer will assume responsibility for the package once it reaches the delivery address.

Orders are processed Monday through Friday, 7:30am - 4:00pm CST. Shipments are prepared in the order that they are received. We make every effort to expedite orders as quickly as possible, but we cannot guarantee ship dates. Please allow up to 3 business days for tracking information. 

Thank you for trusting us with your business.

item.description:
$450 · Very Good · Dave's Gear Depot

Fender (style) Partscaster Jazz Bass. It has a Hybrid neck , 1st 5 frets are med/jumbo brass frets, the remainder of neck has been modified to be Fretless. Fantastic maple grain, truss working ( nut @ body ). Great action, intonation spot on, no neck issues at all its as good as any Fender I've played. Pots are Maxon 1986, pickups Japan made from an 80's J bass. Body Alder unknown make. Looks like a Schaller tail bridge. It is a true sleeper, the tone with these MIJ electronics is outstanding.

item.title: 2015 Ibanez SDB3 Sharlee D'Angelo Signature Electric Bass Guitar! Pearl White! RARE! VERY NICE!!! — $799
item.description:
$799 · Excellent · Top 40 Guitars

At Top 40 Guitars, customer satisfaction is our first concern. All of our items have a 14 day return policy. If after 14 days you are not completely satisfied with your purchase, please return it for a refund....Peace be with you...


*all returned items must be in the same condition they were at the time of sale. All original packing must be included.



Up for Sale is a


2015 Ibanez SDB3 Sharlee D'Angelo Signature Electric Bass Guitar!

Pearl White Finish! Iceman Series!

This is really Cool. You are looking at a 2015 Ibanez SDB3 Sharlee D'Angelo Signature Electric Bass Guitar. Iconic Iceman Series Bass in Pearl White. Sharlee D'Angelo is the bassist for the Melodic Death Metal Band Arch Enemy. 


From The Web:


The Ibanez SDB3 Sharlee D'Angelo Bass, Pearl White is designed to deliver the powerful and aggressive metal-tones of Arch Enemy. The Iceman body shape has been employed to create a radical-looking bass that will make a strong impression on any stage. The PFR high-output pickups give the power required for aggressive metal performance, while the 2-band EQ and balancer control allow you to fully sculpt your sound from onboard the guitar. The guitar has a thin and fast 5-pc maple/walnut neck, fixed with 22 frets which are within easy reach, courtesy of the body's single-cutaway design. An MR4 Mono Rail bridge provides optimal string isolation and ensures that each note will have maximum clarity, despite the low-tuning that this bass comes factory-tuned to. The white and black design with cross inlays gives the SDB3 a sleek aesthetic that is perfect for metal, so if you're looking to make your mark on stage - both tonally and visually - this is the perfect bass to do so.


The SDB3 features an Iceman series mahogany body bolted to a maple and bubinga neck with a 22-fret rosewood fingerboard with iron cross position markers. Components include a pair of Ibanez PFR passive bass pickups, a Mono-rail fixed bridge, a plastic nut, and Ibanez tuning machines. The rear control cover features a reproduction of D'Angelo's signature.


Iceman Body Shape

Sharlee D'Angelo is the Swedish bass player for melodic death metal band, Arch Enemy, among others. His powerful performances require a guitar with a stand-out aesthetic, easy playability, and aggressive tones. The SDB3 signature bass guitar features the angular Iceman body shape to make a real impact, looks-wise. The monochrome design enhances the stand-out aesthetic, with white paint, black body binding, black hardware, and white cross inlays. If you're looking to make an impression on stage, this is the perfect instrument to do so.


Powerful PFR Pickups and 2-Band EQ

The PFR pickups give an aggressively-high output which is perfect for all modern styles of metal. The sound can be blended together using the blend dial, while the 2-band active EQ lets you sculpt your sound to exactly how you require. The active EQ design allows for both boosts and cuts, so you can really take your tone to the next level.


Mono-Rail Bridge

A Mono Rail MR4 bridge provides individual bridge saddles that are fully isolated from one another. This allows you to keep each string independent for maximum clarity in low-end riffing. The MR4 bridge gives the stability and resonance-transfer to make sure that each string rings to its full potential.


Fast Playing Neck

The SDB3 has a thin maple/walnut neck that maintains thickness across its entire length. This is ideal for giving easy reach across all 4 strings, while the 5pc design gives the strength and stability required for maximum sustain and tuning stability. The 12" radius fingerboard is flat-enough to allow a comfortable balance across the strings, while the low-profile neck joint enables easy reach to the very highest frets.

Features:

  • Mahogany body with Iceman body shape
  • 5pc maple/walnut neck
  • Bound Rosewood fingerboard with 22 medium frets
  • MR4 bridge (19mm string spacing)
  • PFR high-output pickups
  • Mono-Rail IV bridge
  • 2-band EQ with treble and bass boost/cut
  • Master volume and pickup balance control

Specifications:

Body:

  • Series: Signature
  • Body Material: Mahogany
  • Construction: Bolt-On

Neck and Fingerboard:

  • Neck Material: 5pc Maple/Walnut
  • Fingerboard Material: Bound Rosewood
  • Inlays: Iron Cross
  • Frets: 22 Medium
  • Scale Length: 864mm/34''
  • Nut Width: 43mm/1.69"
  • Neck Thickness: 22 .5mm at 1st fret, 22.5mm at 12th fret
  • Fingerboard Radius: 305mm

Hardware and Electronics:

  • Pickup Configuration: HH
  • Neck Pickup: PFR-N4 (Black Pole Piece) neck pickup (Passive)
  • Bridge Pickup: PFR-B4 (Black Pole Piece) bridge pickup (Passive)
  • Controls: Vlume, Bass Boost/Cut, Treble Boost/Cut, Balancer
  • Bridge: MR4 Bridge (19mm string spacing)
  • Nut: Half-Bone/Half-brass
  • Hardware Color: Cosmo Black

Our luthier did a complete setup. Brand new strings were installed. Gig ready on Arrival!


Nut Width: 1.69"

Weight: 9.00 lbs.


Case is NOT Included!

Due to CITES Treaty for Endangered Wood (Mahogany, Rosewood) We no longer ship out of the USA.


Cosmetics: Excellent Condition.

  • Top 40 Guitars is a Veteran Owned Business!
  • Thanks For Supporting Veterans!

item.title: Eko 995 Violin Bass - Natural - Project Bass — $325
item.description:
$325 · Poor · drauck

This bass is definitely a project bass with potential.

The bad: The truss rod doesnt seem to affect anything below the 12th fret, it turns and there is tension when it gets tight but it doesn't straighten out, causing the neck to be bowed as shown. My local luthier recommended replacing the truss rod.

The electronics don't currently work, the pickups worked when I got it so the wiring needs to be checked out, should be easy for anyone who knows what they are doing.

Someone installed an additional hole for I gues stereo outputs, then refilled that and the output hole with what looks epoxy putty. It has been sanded down flush with the finish and could be painted to match the finish or covered with a jackplate.

The good: other than the neck the bass is in good shape, minor finish cracks in the finish, very comfortable, working pickups, there is a crack in the headstock that has been repaired and is stable, and it looks cool as hell.

No case but will be shipped securely with loose strings in a gig bag or local pickup in the central Florida area, open to offers, ask any questions prior to purchase please, this is a project vintage guitar.

item.title: Used Ibanez BTB745-NTL 5 String Bass Guitar w/ Bag – Natural Low Gloss — $799
item.description:
$799 · Very Good · GuitarsToBePlayed

Condition: Used, Very Good.

Like what you see All photos are of the exact item you will receive. The bass is in very good shape with signs of play. There are some minor nicks and scratches to the natural wood finish. We’ve included photos so you can take a look!

In via trade! This Ibanez BTB745 5-string is a solid entry in the legendary Ibanez BTB line. This bass has a lot going on for it, especially for the price point. These features include stainless steel frets, Bartolini pickups, a graphite reinforced neck, and the Mono-Rail V bridge. This one is in very good shape with some minor imperfections to the natural wood finish.

From Ibanez: For more than 40 years, Ibanez has pioneered new frontiers in bass development, pushing the boundaries of conventional designs to bass players of all styles, affording new avenues of expression and creativity. This spirit of exploration has led to the creation of some of the most successful and popular production basses of all time. Following this same drive to bring new and relevant ideas to the musical forefront is what serves as the basis for this ongoing project, dubbed the Ibanez Bass Workshop. The impetus behind each one of these unique instruments comes from a desire by the Ibanez bass development team to satisfy the particular needs of many players. These inventions may not be for everyone, but for those who yearn to explore new sonic vistas through the medium of bass: The Ibanez Bass Workshop project may give you just what you're looking for.

 

How Does It Play

The truss rod functions properly and the bass sets up nicely. The string action sits at just over 6/64” on the low B string, and right at 5/64” on the G string, measured at the 12th fret. 

 

Weight: 9lb 6.9oz

 

Non-Original Padded Gigbag included!

 

SPECS

Neck type: BTB55pc Maple/Walnut w/Graphite reinforcement rods/ Neck-through

Top/back/body: Walnut top/ Ash, Okoume wing body

Fretboard: Rosewood fretboard/ Abalone dot inlay

Fret: Medium Stainless-Steel frets

Number of frets: 24 (+ zero fret)

Bridge: MR5 bridge

String space: 19mm

Neck pickup: Bartolini® BH2 neck pickup/ Passive

Bridge pickup: Bartolini® BH2 bridge pickup/ Passive

Equalizer: Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ w/ 3-way Mid frequency switch

Factory tuning: 1G,2D,3A,4E,5B

Hardware color: Cosmo black

NECK DIMENSIONS

Scale :            889mm/35"

a : Width:     47mm at NUT

b : Width:     76mm at 24F

c : Thickness:          20mm at 1F

d : Thickness:         22mm at 12F

Radius :        400mmR

 

Features

 

5pc Maple/Walnut neck with Graphite reinforcement rods

The 5pc Maple/Walnut neck provides tonal clarity and superior attack. The Graphite reinforcement rods provides massive sustain and excellent stability.

 

Rosewood fretboard

Rosewood provides a well-balanced solid tone with a focused mid range.

 

Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ w/Mid frequency switch

Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ is our latest refinement on the concept of simple, straightforward, no-nonsense EQ control. The Boost and Cut for the Mid Control are each centered on slightly different frequencies, an innovation that delivers a preset range of the most favorable bass tones.

 

Bartolini® BH2 pickups

The Bartolini® BH2 pickups provide a tight bottom-end, punchy mid-range, and smooth response. The dual-coil style configuration maximizes articulation and a long blade pole piece widens the magnetic field, providing balanced string-to-string pickup response.

 

MR5 bridge

The Mono-Rail V is the ultimate bridge for bass string isolation. Each bridge is independently and securely locked down to the body for maximum vibration transfer without interference.

item.title: USED Ibanez SR706 (404) — $649.99
item.description:
$649.99 · Very Good · Mountain Music Exchange

Physical Condition
This bass is in great condition, but has been played a bit and shows some light wear. Most notably, there are a handful of scuffs and swirl marks over the body, primarily on the back, along with some moderate tarnishing on the hardware. There's also a bit of dust buildup in the nooks and crannies of the pickups and bridge hardware that could use a quick cleanup during the next restring. Otherwise, there is no other cosmetic damage to make note of aside from some superficial scratches associated with normal play and use. Overall, this is a great sounding and playing bass.

Fretwear
This bass only has some light wear on the first few frets, but no other type of fret damage. Frets and fingerboard could use quick cleanup during the next restring.

Action
Action is medium low and plays great, but may require a quick tweak to your liking.

Electronics
All electronics work properly and sound great.

Case

item.title: Tune Zi-III-5 - 1992 - 5-String Bass - w/ Tune Gigbag - MIJ - Natural Walnut - Ebony Fretboard - Active — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Very Good · Eccentric Guitars

The Zi Series launched in 1988 and at the time served as Tune's top-of-the-line model as well as the step up from the ever popular Bass Maniac series, with both sharing the same core design consisting of a compact body with a slim headstock and reversed tuners, but with the addition of boutique woods and finishes along with a neck-thru build and more robust active electronics.

Here we have a 1992 Zi-III 5-String which was imported from Japan in 2021. These are one of the rarer Tune models, with the 5-strings particularly sought after in Japan.

It features a 5-Piece Walnut, Maple and Bubinga Body, Ebony fretboard, Brass hardware, Dunlop Straploks, and the classic Tune GZP-2 Pre-amp which powers dual Jazz-style blade pickups.

The overall condition of this one is very good. It has typical wear for this style of finish with some fading around where your hands often sit or rub against it, as well as some indents on the pickups from the strings touching them. It's otherwise very clean, especially for its age and it has a beautiful look to it.

Playability-wise it's equally solid. The action is low at around 2.0mm making it easy to play. The neck is straight and all hardware including the truss rod functions perfectly allowing you to adjust it to your preferences. It also weighs in at a comfortable 9.0Ibs.

Included is the original straploks and matching leather gigbag.

item.title: 1963 Fender Precision Bass | Sunburst — $13,000
item.description:
$13,000 · Very Good · The Music Emporium

1963 Fender Precision Bass in original Sunburst finish just into the shop!

Get a load of this thing! We just recently acquired it from the second owner, who'd purchased it directly from the family of the original owner, "Shorty" Nayda. Shorty played bass in Ivan Sexton & The Chicken Coopers, a NJ band who had regional success back in the 1960s and was the house band at Willie's Chicken Coop in Bridgeton NJ. The Chicken Coopers opened up for legendary Country music acts like Conway Twitty and Wanda Jackson!

It's unclear where Shorty bought his bass, but based on some included scans of photographs from the 1960s, he'd had it likely since new.

Stepping into the nuts and bolts of this bass, we discovered that nothing was changed over the years except the pickup foam! The original pickguard, pickup, wiring harness, and hardware remains, and in nice shape at that. The original pickup measures a healthy 12.88k on our meter, which is slightly on the hotter side for pickups from this period. It sounds great, with plenty of punch and definition also responds very well to volume and tone adjustments.

The bass is very light, too. Coming in just under 8.5lbs on our scale, it's incredibly comfortable! It makes more sense now looking back at how a man of Shorty's stature would find a lifelong companion in this Precision. The original brown hardshell case is in great overall condition, with a small amount of padding added to the bottom edge where the lower bout of the body sits. Some of the internal padding/lining had likely failed and was replaced with the DIY padding. Otherwise, the exterior is clean and all the latches work. The handle's in good shape too!

We're including those scans of old photos of Shorty and his band performing, along with a scanned photo of Conway Twitty at the same venue! Such cool provenance!

  • Body//Style--Solidbody, Body--Alder, Finish--Nitrocellulose, Color--3-Tone Sunburst
  • Hardware//Pickups--Original Black Bobbin | 12.88k | Replaced foam, Electronics--Original Stackpole | 304 6309, Controls--Volume / Tone, Bridge--Original, Tuners--Original, Pickguard--Original
  • Neck//Neck--Maple | 5FEB63C Stamp, Binding--None, Fingerboard--Rosewood Veneer, Neck Profile--Oval C | .81"-.98", Inlays--Dots, Nut--1 3/4", Scale Length--34"
  • Other//Serial #--95393, Weight--8lbs 7oz, Case--Original Hardshell

________________________________________

The Music Emporium has been a fixture of the Boston music community since 1968. We're a shop built by musicians - players, luthiers, photographers, and gear obsessives - and that shapes everything about how we do business.

Every instrument we ship is inspected and set up by our in-house guitar techs before it leaves the shop.

We're a Fender Showcase Dealer, Collings' largest dealer worldwide, and a Martin Custom Shop Expert Dealer. Our showroom is at 165 Massachusetts Ave in Lexington, MA.

Questions about any instrument are always welcome. We're happy to talk gear.

item.title: Fender Player Plus Precision Bass with Pau Ferro Fretboard in 3-Color Sunburst 2021 — $649.99
item.description:
$649.99 · Excellent · The Guitar Department

This bass is in overall excellent condition. The neck is straight and the frets show minimal wear. The electronics function properly and quietly. There are some minor cosmetic blemishes. There are some very light scuffs/scratches on the body. The top has a couple small dings toward the left edge of the body. The back of the body has a small ding toward the center. There is a small finish crack on the bass side of the neck pocket. Please reference the photos for physical condition, as they are part of the item description.


This bass weighs 8lb 15oz.


Building on the foundation of their Player Bass series, Fender is pleased to introduce the all-new Player Plus Active Precision Bass. With an expanded 3-band EQ for flexible midrange tone shaping, bassists love that the Player Plus supplies even more of the patented punch and growl that Fender basses are known for. The mini toggle switches between active and passive circuitry, ensuring that your signal is as strong as the groovy lines you're laying down. A Jazz Bass bridge pickup adds bite while the HiMass bridge intensifies your sustain and resonance. The contemporary "C"-shaped neck is topped with a comfy 12-inch radius fingerboard with rolled edges for fluid runs up the neck. Pick out your favorite Player Plus finish, plug in, and you'll see and hear the remarkable difference of this Active Precision Bass.


  • 3-band EQ for more prominent midrange tone shaping
  • Active/passive toggle for fast and efficient signal intensity switching
  • Noiseless Jazz Bass Bridge and PJ single coil pickups for reduced signal buzz
  • Solid HiMass bridge for improved sustain and intonation
  • 12-inch fingerboard radius and "C"-shaped maple neck for more comfortable, modern feel
  • Rolled-edge pau ferro fingerboard keeps playing smooth and efficient
  • Classic-look nickel/chrome finished hardware
  • Durable 3-ply pick guard and stunning gloss polyester finish

*QM*

item.description:
$350 · Good · Music Go Round Toledo

Condition: Good

Accessories/Disclaimer:

Only accessories specifically listed in the Includes Case/Gig Bag section or shown in the photos are included. No additional accessories, straps, cables, tools, manuals, cases, gig bags, or other items are included unless pictured or stated.


Used Ibanez ATK3EX1 4-String Active Bass Guitar Black 2007 - Modified Electronics


The Ibanez ATK series has earned a reputation for delivering huge low-end punch, outstanding sustain, and versatile modern bass tones. This 2007 ATK3EX1 features the signature ATK body design, comfortable maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, and the powerful triple-coil bridge pickup that has made the series a favorite among bass players for decades.


This bass has been modified with upgraded active electronics that require two 9-volt batteries. It features two mini-toggle switches and an LED indicator that are not believed to be original equipment. The exact functions of the switches have not been verified. During testing, the bass functions properly in active mode; however, one switch position does not produce output.


Features:

• 2007 Ibanez ATK3EX1

• Made in Indonesia

• Black gloss finish

• Maple neck

• Rosewood fingerboard

• 22 frets

• Triple-coil ATK bridge pickup

• Modified active electronics

• Two 9V battery operation

• Two mini-toggle switches

• LED indicator

• Heavy-duty ATK bridge


Condition:

This bass is in good overall condition and has been tested. It performs well in active mode and delivers the powerful, aggressive tone the ATK series is known for.


Cosmetically, the instrument shows moderate signs of normal use, including light scratches, swirl marks, and finish wear throughout the body. The hardware has light cosmetic oxidation, and a few screw heads show minor surface rust. The neck is straight, the frets have plenty of life remaining, and the bass plays comfortably.


Please note that the electronics have been modified from the factory configuration. The onboard electronics require two 9V batteries, and while the bass operates properly in active mode, one switch position does not produce output. Because the electronics have been modified, the exact function of each switch is unknown.


Store Policies:

• All used equipment is tested before being offered for sale and again prior to shipping.

• Ships the next business day.

• Continental U.S. shipping only.

• No trades on shipped items.

• Returns accepted within 48 hours of delivery. Buyer is responsible for return shipping, and a 20% restocking fee applies.

• Please feel free to contact us with any questions before purchasing.

item.title: Dingwall NG3 5-String Ferrari Green Fanned Fret Multi-Scale w/ 12-Way Programmable Rotary 9.18lbs #19111 — $3,395
item.description:
$3,395 · Brand New · Fret Nation

Dingwall NG3 5-String Ferrari Green Electric Bass w/ Dingwall Bag - IN STOCK!

  • Part Number: NG3 5 FG M MH
  • Serial Number: 19111
  • Weight: 9.18 Pounds

This bass features the new McGrath Guitars Programmable Rotary with up to 12 programmable settings! 

The new McGrath 12-Way Rotary Selector allows for massive tonal versatility allowing you to quickly recall your favorite setting and go back to the last one you were on!

Rotary Settings:

  • All Parallel
  • Bridge Solo
  • Middle Solo
  • Neck Solo
  • Bridge & Middle in Parallel
  • Middle & Neck in Parallel
  • Bridge & Neck in Parallel
  • Bridge & Middle in Series
  • Middle & Neck in Series
  • Bridge & Neck in Series
  • Bridge & Middle in Series in Parallel with Neck
  • All Series

Save: Long Press to save your current setting

Recall: Push Once to jump to your saved favorite.

Recall Last: Push to return to your previous setting.

Reset: Triple Tap to return to Position 1 (All Parallel)

Back by popular demand!  Ferrari Green!  This refreshed Ferrari Green Dingwall will feature a maple fingerboard with binding!  The binding gives the fingerboard edges a smoother feel!


Software coming very soon to allow you to program your preferred pickup settings.

The Dingwall NG Adam "Nolly" Getgood Signature basses are based on the Dingwall Combustion. The Dingwall NG3 features an alder body, maple neck, maple fingerboard, a custom Darkglass Tone Capsule pre-amp, sweetspot pickup placement, carbon fiber print pickguard and sharp racing inspired finishes.  

Dingwall Bag Included

Dingwall NG 5-String Specifications:

  • Body Core: Alder  

Alder is the traditional wood for the electric bass. It can't be beat for a traditional warm tone with strong forward mids, controlled highs and warm but not muddy lows.

Finish: The satin and gloss NG finishes are polyester base topped with polyurethane color and top coats. This industry standard finish is long-wearing and beautiful for years of life.
Colors:

  • Ferrari Green Gloss
  • Pickguard: Carbon fiber print - black

Neck Specifications:

Dingwall takes neck construction very seriously due to their extensive experience in guitar repairs and understanding of extreme climates. Dingwall experimented with many different lamination techniques and have found a 5-piece maple construction to be among the best.

  • Neck Carve: Medium thin C shape
  • Neck Reinforcement: Carbon fiber reinforcements and 2-way truss rod
  • Headstock: Flat with string retaining bar.  

Dingwall has combined the minimalist shape of the Afterburner headstock with the lightest import tuners they could find. The result is an excellent balance and solid performance.

  • Fingerboard: Bound Maple

Maple has a nice snappy top end that really enhances the attack of the note. Dingwall now finishes the NG maple fingerboards with a satin polyurethane to maintain stability. The entire neck is finished with the same finish.

  • Fingerboard radius: 240mm (9.45")

The NG bass features a single radius fingerboard. This makes for a very comfortable playing experience.

Fret Material and Size: Dingwall uses 18% hard nickel silver fretwire.  

Lee Sklar turned Dingwall onto the benefits of small frets. The advantages are a more woody, less metallic tone, smoother glissando and a faster feel. Dingwalls standard size is "banjo" size. This is the same size you would find on most guitars from the 50's and 60's. Although its size is smaller than standard bass fretwire, its service life is about the same.

Position Markers and Inlays: 3mm plastic face dots, 2mm plastic side dots

Dingwall NG Electronics:

  • Pickups: FD-3N pickups made to Dingwalls specifications

Featuring Neodymium magnets which offer high output and a smooth yet aggressive tone.

  • 3-Pickup:
  • Master Volume

McGrath 12-Way Programmable Rotary

  • All Parallel
  • Bridge Solo
  • Middle Solo
  • Neck Solo
  • Bridge & Middle in Parallel
  • Middle & Neck in Parallel
  • Bridge & Neck in Parallel
  • Bridge & Middle in Series
  • Middle & Neck in Series
  • Bridge & Neck in Series
  • Bridge & Middle in Series in Parallel with Neck
  • All Series


3-Band Darkglass pre-amp w/ Active/Passive toggle switch

Knobs: Black Knurled Dome Top

Jack: The jack is one of the most critical electronic components of a bass. It is the most likely to suffer abuse so needs to be rugged in order to be reliable. Dingwall wanted the Combustion to be as reliable as it is great sounding and looking so Dingwall installs an amazing socket style jack plate with a Switchcraft brand jack. This is the most reliable system you will find in this price range.

Dingwall NG Hardware Specifications:

  • Hardware Color: Black
  • Tuners: Custom color HIPSHOT Ultralight Tuners
  • Bridge: Dingwall Minimalist Bridge
  • Strap Buttons: Standard

Dingwall NG Scale Length and Dimensions:

Scale Lengths:

B-String = 37.00"
E-String = 36.25"
A-String = 35.50"
D-String = 34.75"
G-String = 34.00"

  • Bridge Spacing: 18mm
  • Nut Width: 45.5mm / 1.80"
  • Heel Width: 75mm / 2.95"
  • Neck Thickness @ 1st Fret: 21mm / .827"
  • Neck Thickness @ 12th Fret: 24mm / .945"

item.title: Regal PBass — $399
item.description:
$399 · Good · Jared's Custom Guitar Shop

This guitar has been tested and all electronics are in working order.

Missing one tuners bushing, but works properly.

My focus was using it to create a piece of art; I consider this essentially a working, playable piece of art. It is currently showing in my St. Louis art gallery, 31art gallery.

The overall body, the area of white is the factory finish. The other areas were painted by me personally.

The portions of the guitar that I painted are no longer factory finish and therefore would not hold up to the same protection as a factory finish. I used spray paints and oil based paints. The paints hold up great to general playing/use, but is more susceptible to scratching than a factory finish. It won't rub off and can be cleaned with a light cleaner, like Windex. No clear coat has been applied.

I have the action set up at 4/32 to 3/32 at 12. Oiled fretboard. Adjusted truss rod. Sounds great. Ready to play!!!

Gear up and get ready to take your performance to the next level with Player II Modified. Featuring key upgrades for the modern guitarist and bassist, these versatile instruments are designed to handle anything you can throw at them.

The Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass® is a classic bass with the modern player in mind. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern C-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5-radius slab rosewood or 1-piece maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay Special Soda Pink Sparkle MP w/case — $2,799
item.description:
$2,799 · Brand New · Tone Shop Guitars

Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay Special Soda Pink Sparkle MP

Overview
The Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay Special refines the classic StingRay formula with modern updates aimed at improved balance, playability, and headroom. Built around a select hardwood body and a roasted maple neck, this model delivers the familiar punch and clarity StingRays are known for while offering a smoother, more responsive feel across the neck.

A single neodymium humbucker feeds an 18-volt active preamp, providing strong output, clean dynamics, and plenty of tonal control. Finished in Soda Pink Sparkle, this StingRay Special pairs bold visual character with a straightforward, professional-grade bass designed to sit confidently in a mix.

Highlights

  • Single neodymium humbucker with classic StingRay voice
  • 18V active 3-band preamp for increased headroom and clarity
  • Roasted maple neck with oil and wax finish for stability and smooth feel
  • Stainless steel frets for long-term durability
  • Soda Pink Sparkle high-gloss polyester finish

Specifications

Body

  • Model: StingRay Special
  • Body Wood: Select Hardwoods
  • Body Finish: High gloss polyester
  • Dimensions: 13-1/2" wide x 1-5/8" thick x 44-7/8" long
  • Scale Length: 34"

Neck

  • Neck Wood: Select Roasted Maple
  • Fingerboard: Maple / Rosewood / Ebony (dependent on body color)
  • Neck Finish: Gunstock oil and hand-rubbed special wax blend
  • Neck Radius: 11"
  • Frets: 22 High Profile, Wide Stainless Steel
  • Neck Width: 1-11/16" at nut, 2-1/2" at last fret
  • Headstock Length: 8-3/4"
  • Neck Colors: Natural with finished headstock
  • Truss Rod: Adjustable (no component or string removal required)
  • Neck Attachment: 5-bolt sculpted neck joint

Hardware

  • Bridge: Vintage Music Man top-loaded chrome plated steel bridge plate with vintage nickel plated steel saddles
  • Tuning Machines: Custom Music Man lightweight tuners with tapered string posts and ergonomic clover design
  • Electronic Shielding: Chrome or black plated aluminum control cover (dependent on body color)

Electronics

  • Pickups: Single Humbucking pickup with Neodymium magnets
  • Controls: Volume, Treble, Mid, Bass
  • Preamp: 3-band active preamp with 18 volts of headroom
  • Switching: Single pickup (no selector)

Miscellaneous

  • Strings: 45–65–80–100 (Super Slinky Bass #2834)

item.description:

Maruszczyk Instruments Jazzus 5a – Custom 5-String Bass (2021) | Transparent Bishop | Made in Poland

Up for sale is a stunning Maruszczyk Instruments Jazzus 5a, a premium custom-built 5-string bass handcrafted in Poland. Completed in September 2021, this instrument combines exceptional craftsmanship, versatile electronics, and modern playability with a beautiful Transparent Bishop finish over a figured burl poplar top.

The bass features an ash body with a striking burl poplar top, paired with a one-piece maple neck reinforced with graphite rods for excellent stability. The very dark katalox fingerboard provides a smooth playing experience, while the Delano Xtender 5HE/S pickups and Darkglass Tone Capsule preamp deliver everything from vintage warmth to aggressive modern tones.

Each pickup includes its own series/parallel mini switch, offering a wide range of tonal options beyond a standard Jazz-style bass.

item.description:

Title: 1972 Fender Telecaster Bass - 9.2 lbs - Flawless Neck & '72 Seth Lover Pickup w/Original Orange-Interior Case

Description: Up for sale is a phenomenal, gig-ready 1972 Fender Telecaster Bass that balances incredible vintage mojo with effortless playability. Weighing in at a highly desirable, comfortable 9.2 pounds, this solid ash bass hits the perfect sweet spot for sustain and resonance without the back-breaking weight of later 70s Fenders.This bass has all the vintage pedigree where it counts—the neck, the wood, and that monstrous '72 Seth Lover pickup.

The Details:

  • Weight: 9.2 lbs (Perfect vintage weight with incredible sustain).
  • Body: Solid Ash. Originally finished in Black, it was professionally refinished to Natural, beautifully showcasing the vintage ash wood grain. It has very few minor dings and honest, light play wear.
  • Neck & Frets: 100% authentic, stamped by legendary Fender craftsman J. Torres on the heel. The neck profile is absolutely perfect, straight, and comfortable. The truss rod functions smoothly, and the original frets are in excellent shape. It plays cleanly up and down the board with low, comfortable action.
  • Electronics: 100% vintage and untouched. Features the legendary, massive-sounding Seth Lover Wide Range humbucker, explicitly dated to 1972.
  • Modifications: Non-original pickguard; old body refinish (originally black, now natural).
  • Case: Includes the original 1972 Fender hardshell case. The exterior Fender logo and the iconic orange plush interior are both in excellent, condition with all working latches.

Shipping & Terms:
Ships securely packed inside its original hardshell case and fully insured. Please ask all questions before purchasing!

item.title: X Series Spectra SBXP 5 String - Transparent Purple - USED — $525
item.description:
$525 · Very Good · Ernie Williamson Music

***On display at our Joplin, Mo location***

Jackson X Series Spectra Bass SBXP V – Transparent Purple Burst

The Jackson X Series Spectra Bass SBXP V is a premium bass designed for players seeking exceptional tone, playability, and style. With modern design, flexible electronics, and incomparable value, this instrument takes the adventurous player on a bold bass odyssey. Featuring a poplar body with a stunning poplar burl top, a graphite-reinforced through-body maple neck, and high-performance electronics, this instrument delivers versatility and professional sound for the progressive bassist. The Jackson X Series Spectra Bass SBXP V is finished in a standout Transparent Purple Burst, making it a visually striking choice for any stage.

Key Features:
  • Body Material: Poplar body with a poplar burl top provides a meaty, resonant tone with a distinguished upper midrange, while the burl adds a distinctive wood grain pattern.
  • Neck Construction: Through-body maple neck with graphite reinforcement and a scarf joint creates formidable sustain and rock-solid stability.
  • Fingerboard: 12"-16" compound radius bound laurel fingerboard with 24 jumbo frets ensures superior playability and comfort across the entire neck.
  • Pickup Configuration: Dual passive medium-output humbucking pickups deliver a dynamic foundation with punch, power, and note articulation.
  • Electronics & Control Layout: Features a two-way toggle switch for pickup coil splitting, a 3-band active EQ, a blend control, and a volume control with a push/pull selector to engage or bypass the active circuit.
  • Bridge System: Jackson HiMass hardtail bridge enhances sustain and improves note attack for a more defined sound.
  • Scale Length: 35" scale length provides optimal string tension and clarity for the five-string configuration.
  • Nut Width: 1.75" nut width offers a comfortable and precise playing feel.
  • Tuning Machines: Jackson sealed die-cast tuners ensure reliable tuning stability.
  • Headstock: Unique 3x2 color-matched headstock features an understated J logo and complements the instrument's overall aesthetic.
  • Finish: Transparent Purple Burst finish combined with black hardware creates a bold, modern look.

Built with Jackson’s signature ergonomic design and cutting-edge technology, the Jackson X Series Spectra Bass SBXP V redefines modern bass performance. Combining a balanced offset body, superior tonal clarity, and effortless playability, this instrument exemplifies the innovation and craftsmanship that set Jackson apart. Whether you are exploring complex progressive lines or driving the rhythm with power and punch, this bass is ready for your next musical journey.


Why buy from Ernie Williamson Music?

Ernie Williamson Music is a full-line music store offering sales, service, rentals and the best lesson programs in the area. We are a "Best of the Ozarks" award winner, ranked in the top 100 music stores in the US, the number one Taylor Guitar dealer in Missouri, and a top 20 Taylor Guitar dealer in the US. We're a locally owned store with friendly, knowledgeable staff—but we make sure our prices compete with the big box stores. Whether you shop online or come by one of our four regional locations, you'll get excellent service, quality gear, and low prices.

item.title: Sterling by Music Man SUB Series StingRay RAY4 HH Electric Bass Guitar - Regular Trans Black Satin — $307.19
item.description:
$307.19 · Good · Musician's Friend

** Item Specific Details ** The top of the body is cracked around the pickup selector switch mounting holes and travel into the control cavity. There are scuffs on the top and back of the body. **

The Sterling by Music Man SUB Series StingRay RAY4 HH electric bass blends classic StingRay character with the expanded versatility of a dual-humbucker design. Outfitted with two ceramic pickups, a dedicated 9V active preamp and a five-way selector, this model delivers a wide palette of punchy, articulate tones suited for rehearsal, recording or the stage. A slim 1.5" nut width and hard maple neck create a fast, comfortable feel, while open-gear tuners and a fixed bridge ensure stable tuning and solid sustain. For players who want the familiar StingRay feel combined with flexible pickup switching and expressive output, the RAY4 HH offers a reliable, performance-ready platform.

Dual Ceramic Humbuckers Expand Tonal Range

Two ceramic humbuckers form the core of the RAY4 HH’s voice, each engineered for clarity, high output and noise rejection. Their placement gives the instrument strong low-end presence, defined mids and the punch needed to cut through dense mixes. Whether laying down rhythmic foundations or exploring melodic lines, the humbuckers maintain consistent articulation and presence across dynamic shifts.

Active 9V Preamp Shapes Output and Response

An onboard 9V active preamp boosts signal strength and enhances overall definition, working in tandem with the ceramic pickups to deliver sharper attack and longer sustain. The two-band EQ provides direct control over bass and treble, making it easy to add weight, brighten the top end or fine-tune your sound for different environments. This active system gives the StingRay HH model ample headroom and lets players balance deep lows and crisp highs with confidence.

Five-Way Selector Offers On-the-Fly Pickup Switching

The five-way selector enables rapid tonal changes, blending the dual humbuckers in configurations that range from full, powerful dual-coil output to tighter, more focused single-coil-style tones. Positioned for quick access, the switch allows players to shift character mid-song without interrupting their groove. The result is a bass that adapts easily to different playing approaches, musical styles or performance settings.

Solid Construction Supports Long-Term Playability

A bayur body and hard maple neck provide a stable, resonant foundation, while the jatoba fingerboard with white-dot markers offers smooth playability and clear visibility. The six-bolt neck joint enhances structural integrity and sustain, and open-gear tuners maintain reliable tuning accuracy. Paired with a fixed bridge that improves string vibration transfer and intonation, the RAY4 HH is built to withstand travel, heavy practice schedules and regular gigging.

Level Up Your Tone With the StingRay RAY4 HH

Players who value hands-on tone shaping and the unmistakable StingRay feel will appreciate the RAY4 HH’s responsive electronics and comfortable build. Its switching system, active preamp and ceramic humbuckers provide the punch, clarity and versatility needed for tight grooves, expressive phrasing and dependable low-end presence. Whether you're developing new ideas in the studio or locking in live, this model translates technique into powerful, controlled sound.

item.title: Fender American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass Left-Handed Regular Sea Foam Green — $1,599.99
item.description:
$1,599.99 · Good · Musician's Friend

** Item Specific Details ** Couple finish chips, top bass side bout and back upper treble side bout. Finish crack at headstock, G string post. SN - V2210148 ** The Fender American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass left-handed bass guitar celebrates the creation of the original—the result of Don Randall at Fender Sales requesting a more sophisticated, high-end bass. The neck was narrower at the nut, providing a faster and more effortless feel, and the sleek new body style mirrored the offset contoured style of the Jazzmaster. By 1966, evolution of the Jazz Bass reached new heights with striking new features.

The American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass features a comfortable U-shaped hard rock maple neck, and it plays like a dream with a 7.25"-radius round-lam rosewood fingerboard and vintage tall frets. Characteristic of production in 1966, this J Bass is offered with white neck binding, pearl dots, two volume controls with master tone, matching painted headstock and a 4-ply pickguard. The 4-saddle bridge with threaded saddles, vintage-style “lollipop” tuners and Pure Vintage ’66 Jazz Bass pickups deliver classic thump and style. It comes with a vintage-style black case with orange interior.

The Fender American Vintage II series presents a remarkably accurate take on the revolutionary designs that altered the course of musical history. Built with period-accurate bodies, necks and hardware, premium finishes and meticulously voiced, year-specific pickups, each instrument captures the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.

item.title: AIO Wolf S11-7 7-String Bass Guitar - Wine Red 008 — $749
item.description:
$749 · Brand New · All In One Guitar [AIO Custom & Wolf]

You will be receiving the exact guitar pictured.

We will install a New alkaline Duracell battery and will Setup with a New Set of D'addario XL Nickel Wound Strings ( 18-32-45-65-100-130 guage ).

To make sure you get the best-tailored setup, below are the setup options you can choose from, and also, please let us know your level of playing/playing style and your age(Just curious for our records :))

1. Medium Action 4/32 inch on treble side5/32 on bass side
2. Medium Low Action 3/32 -4/32
3.Low Action (more low on bass side) 2.5/32- 3 /32

Also, Please choose the neck relief option as well.

A. Traditional Style(more like Fender type)
B. More relief so the low to high is very even.

**Please note, for setup #3, weather and temperature changes during shipping could affect our setup(buzzing issue). And if you play it with heavy stroke with #3 setup then there will be a buzz on the fingerboard. Generally, when you get your instrument it might be slightly changed(Intonation, neck relief, and fret level) during the delivery with the carrier, especially the tuning.To To make sure you get the best-tailored setup, below are the setup options you can choose from, and also, please let us know your level of playing/playing style and your age(Just curious for our records

This stunning S11 7-string bass is one of our absolute favorites. Not only does it look incredible, but its playability and tone are superb. Expect warm, tight low-end, silky highs, and an even sound throughout the neck. Each bass undergoes a professional setup before it leaves our shop, ensuring it feels comfortable in your hands. The ebony fingerboard adds a growl and punch that's hard to beat. Buy with confidence!

Specifications

  • Body: Constructed from Solid African Mahogany, providing a deep, rich tone with excellent sustain. Topped and backed with Solid Flame Maple, this bass not only sounds incredible but also looks stunning in its finish.
  • Neck: Solid Maple Neck-Through design reinforced with Solid Walnut (4 pieces Maple and 3 pieces Walnut). This configuration offers superb stability and sustain, making it a joy to play.
  • Fingerboard: Sleek Ebony, delivering a smooth and responsive playing experience.
  • Headstock: Topped with Solid Flame Maple, adding a touch of elegance and visual appeal. 
  • Top and Back: Solid Flame Maple
  • Position Marks: Side dots to keep your playing on point. 
  • Tuners: Grover tuners for reliable tuning stability, ensuring your instrument stays perfectly in tune.
  • Truss Rod: Dual action, allowing for precise neck adjustments to suit your playing style. 
  • Scale Length: 35''
  • Battery: Powered by dual 9V batteries, providing consistent and reliable performance.
  • Neck thickness at !st Fret:  22.9 mm
  • Neck thickness at 12th Fret: 24.33 mm 
  • Bridge width: 3 7/8'' 
  • Bridge Spacing: 15 mm 
  • Nut Width: 60.9 mm
  • Width at the 12th Fret: 86mm
  •  Pick Up: AIO Custom Single Space Mini Humbucker (Coiled by Accurate CNC machine) 
  • Lower Bout: 11''
  • Upper Bout: 13.5" 
  • Total Length of Bass: 46.5"  

Controls

2-Position Mini Toggle Switch: Selector for on and off the volume

Master Volume push/pull for active or passive

Center Tapped Front and Rear Pickup Mix Volume

Center Tapped Bass (only works at an Active position)

Center Tapped Middle (only works at an Active position)

Center Tapped Treble (only works at an Active position)


About All in One Guitars

We run a humble one-stop guitar shop in Los Angeles. We are a team of great guitar/bass players, builders and repairers. The owner of AIO and all of the staff and luthiers are professional musicians with 30-40 years of experience. You can find many guitars/basses anywhere but we are confident that the ones from our shop are better than the ones you get from big corporate shops. Anyone that has done business with All in One Guitars can confirm that we focus on playability so our setup standard is incredibly high. Our guitar set-ups are custom shop quality and the playability and feel is off the charts. You are paying not just for the price of the instrument, but also for our close attention and care for it. We deal with honesty and integrity and we make sure that the instrument feels good in your hands. We want to share our quality instruments and let everyone enjoy and love music with the ease of playing.

We hope that you can make purchases with the confidence of knowing that not a single instrument leaves our store without receiving our utmost care.
